workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Just got this from David Rivera at Fourth Coffee -- I need help figuring out security posture. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 12:22 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 11:10 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee security posture

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 10:06 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee security posture

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-J trap (Forecast/risk update missed when escalation warrants); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-J failure mode (Forecast/risk update missed when escalation warrants)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-J -- Forecast/risk update missed when escalation warrants
